data.title: After 10 months of silence, a whale withdrew 1,870 XAUT tokens from OKX, worth $9.54 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale withdrew 1,870 XAUT tokens from OKX after 10 months of inactivity, worth $9.54 million.

data.title: Iron Ore Prices Surge Amid China's Restrictions on BHP Products
data.description:
Iron ore is on track for its largest weekly increase in more than a year, driven by China's state-backed buyer imposing expanded restrictions on BHP products. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of these restrictions on the global iron ore market. The move by China, a major player in the iron ore industry, has led to a significant rise in prices, reflecting the market's sensitivity to supply chain disruptions.

The restrictions are part of China's broader strategy to manage its domestic market and ensure stable supply chains. This development has caused a ripple effect across the global iron ore market, with traders and investors closely monitoring the situation.

Market analysts suggest that the restrictions could lead to further volatility in iron ore prices, as supply constraints may persist. The situation underscores the importance of China's role in the global commodities market and its influence on pricing dynamics.

As the week progresses, stakeholders in the iron ore industry will be watching for any further announcements or policy changes from China that could impact the market. The current surge in prices highlights the interconnected nature of global supply chains and the potential for geopolitical actions to affect commodity markets.

data.title: KiiChain Secures $22 Million in Funding Led by Nimbus Capital
data.description:
KiiChain, a blockchain-based foreign exchange trading platform, has successfully raised $22 million in funding as of January 14. According to Foresight News, the funding round was led by Nimbus Capital, with participation from Super Cycle Capital, WTG Ventures, and others.

KiiChain aims to offer global payment solutions, round-the-clock foreign exchange services, and financial infrastructure to businesses and users in emerging markets.

data.title: Japan's Finance Minister Katayama Gozuki Urges Caution Amid High Oil Prices
data.description: Japan's Finance Minister Katayama Gozuki has expressed concern over the persistently high oil prices, emphasizing the need for caution in economic planning. According to Jin10, the minister highlighted the potential impact of elevated oil costs on the country's economic stability and urged careful monitoring of the situation. The ongoing fluctuations in oil prices could pose challenges to Japan's economic recovery efforts, necessitating strategic adjustments to mitigate adverse effects. The government is expected to closely observe global market trends and consider appropriate measures to address the issue.

data.title: America Movil Eyes Expansion with Grupo Salinas Acquisition in Colombia
data.description: Mexico's telecommunications giant, America Movil, is reportedly in talks to acquire certain telecom operations from Grupo Salinas in Colombia. Bloomberg posted on X that this move is part of America Movil's strategy to broaden its presence in the Latin American market. The acquisition would potentially enhance America Movil's service offerings and market share in the region, aligning with its growth objectives. Further details on the negotiations and potential deal terms have not been disclosed.

data.title: ElizaOS Founder Criticizes Burwick Law Over Lack of Fund Recovery
data.description: ElizaOS founder Shaw has publicly criticized Burwick Law for failing to recover any funds for victims. According to Foresight News, Shaw accused the law firm of defaming the project by claiming it did not hold any token supply until receiving donations. In response, Burwick Law stated it would retain all tweets from Shaw that attack or threaten the firm.

data.title: Solana Superteam USA Launches to Boost Ecosystem Growth
data.description: On March 13, Solana Superteam USA announced its official launch, aiming to accelerate the involvement of entrepreneurs across the United States in the Solana ecosystem. According to BlockBeats, the initiative is led by Nicky Scanz, who is also the founder of the decentralized validation project Layer33 and the host of the podcast The Index Show. Scanz began his journey in the Solana community with NFT projects before shifting focus to ecosystem development and strategic consulting. Currently, he is dedicated to fostering the growth of the Solana builder community in the U.S.

data.title: Global Leaders Release Emergency Oil Reserves Amid Persian Gulf Conflict
data.description: World leaders have agreed to release emergency petroleum stockpiles as conflict in the Persian Gulf disrupts oil exports. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the decision as a response to the escalating situation that threatens global energy supplies. The move aims to stabilize markets and ensure adequate supply amid rising tensions in the region. The release of these reserves is seen as a crucial step to mitigate potential shortages and price spikes. The Persian Gulf, a key oil-producing region, has been a focal point of geopolitical tensions, impacting the flow of oil to international markets. This decision underscores the importance of maintaining energy security and the readiness of nations to act in times of crisis. The situation continues to evolve, with global leaders closely monitoring developments to assess further actions needed to safeguard energy supplies.

data.title: The US government says it will take 4,431,161 hours to process tariff refunds.
data.description: Following the U.S. Supreme Court's ruling last month that the U.S. government's massive tariffs were illegal, numerous American companies quickly filed lawsuits seeking to recover their payments. However, the U.S. government subsequently stated that there was a problem: it could take up to 4,431,161 hours (approximately 506 years) to manually process all refund requests. (The New York Times)

data.title: White House Challenges ABC News Report on Iran Drone Threat
data.description: The White House has called for ABC News to retract a report concerning a potential Iranian drone attack on California, citing reliance on an unverified source. According to NS3.AI, the controversy arose after an FBI bulletin, leaked in February, described a scenario involving a retaliatory drone strike from a vessel off the West Coast. Additionally, the article mentioned that U.S. prosecutors aimed to seize over $500,000 in USDT in September, linked to a separate case involving Iranian drone technology.

data.title: POAP to Cease New Service Development Amid Market Constraints
data.description: POAP announced that it will stop developing new services starting March 16, focusing instead on maintaining its current systems. According to NS3.AI, General Manager Isabel Gonzalez stated that the platform encountered challenges in expanding beyond its niche market. Despite this shift, existing users and interfaces will continue to receive support.

data.title: Bitcoin Surpasses $70,000 Amid AI Sector Gains
data.description: The cryptocurrency market experienced widespread gains, with notable increases across various sectors. According to NS3.AI, the AI sector saw a significant rise of 7.24% over the past 24 hours. Bitcoin also made headlines by breaking the $70,000 mark during this upward movement.

data.title: Whale Activity Sparks Debate Over Ethereum's Future
data.description:
Analyst @ai_9684xtpa posted on X about the ongoing debate between bullish and bearish sentiments in the Ethereum market. On one side, there are speculations that a prominent figure, referred to as 'Yi Boss,' might be shorting Ethereum by borrowing coins. On the other side, a whale with the wallet address 0x7099c7d7fCa074062a0fc593A35F788605bCad6e has made a significant move after four months of inactivity. This whale purchased 10,557.14 ETH at an average price of $2,072.55 using 20.058 million sUSDS.

The market is keenly observing these developments to determine which side will prevail in this ongoing tug-of-war. The actions of these major players could potentially influence Ethereum's price trajectory in the near future.

data.title: Singapore's AI Ambitions Face Readiness Challenges
data.description: Singapore has invested over S$1 billion ($782 million) to establish itself as a global leader in artificial intelligence. Bloomberg posted on X that despite this significant financial commitment, concerns have been raised about the country's preparedness for AI integration. One of the key figures behind Singapore's AI strategy has expressed doubts about whether the current approach is adequate to meet the challenges and opportunities presented by AI advancements. The nation aims to leverage AI to boost its economy and technological standing, but the effectiveness of its readiness strategy remains under scrutiny.

data.title: Crypto sectors generally rose, with AI stocks leading the gains at over 7%, and BTC breaking through $70,000.
data.description: On March 13th, according to SoSoValue data, the crypto market generally saw gains. The AI ​​sector performed strongly, rising 7.24% in the last 24 hours, with Fetch.ai (FET) up 16.86%, Render (RENDER) up 14.83%, and Bittensor (TAO) up 13.02%. Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC) rose 2.53%, surpassing $71,000; Ethereum (ETH) rose 3.78%, surpassing $2,100. (http://fetch.ai/) Notably, MAG7.ssi rose 2.74%, DEFI.ssi rose 2.95%, and MEME.ssi rose 4.33%. In other sectors, the Meme sector rose 4.70%, with Pump.fun (PUMP) up 8.08%; the DeFi sector rose 3.80%, with River (RIVER) up 24.97%; the Layer 2 sector rose 3.13%, with Starknet (STRK) up 5.51%; the Layer 1 sector rose 2.91%, with NEAR Protocol (NEAR) up 9.29%; the PayFi sector rose 2.40%, with Dash (DASH) up 3.44%; and the CeFi sector rose 1.75%, with Mantle (MNT) up 4.75%. Crypto sector indices reflecting historical sector performance show that the ssiAI, ssiRWA, and ssiDePIN indices rose 7.32%, 6.08%, and 5.42%, respectively.

data.title: Regal Hedge Fund Faces Setback as Immutep Halts Lung Cancer Drug Trials
data.description: Regal, a Sydney-based hedge fund, encountered a significant challenge following Immutep's decision to cease trials of its lung cancer drug. Bloomberg posted on X that the Australian biotech firm, supported by Regal, saw a substantial reduction in its market value, erasing most of its A$582 million ($412 million) valuation. The halt in trials has impacted investor confidence, leading to a sharp decline in the company's stock value.

data.title: US Unemployment Rate Expected to Rise Amid Small Business Challenges
data.description:
The Kobeissi Letter posted on X. The U.S. unemployment rate is anticipated to increase as 11% of small businesses in the country reported 'poor sales' as their primary concern in February. This figure is near the highest since 2020 and ranks as the fourth most cited issue, following taxes, quality of labor, and inflation. Over the past three years, the percentage of businesses citing poor sales has tripled, aligning with levels seen during past recessions.

Historically, such trends have been a precursor to rising unemployment, given that small businesses are the largest employers in the economy. This pattern suggests that the unemployment rate could exceed 5% in the coming months, up from the current 4.4%. If realized, this would mark the highest unemployment rate since mid-2021, during the pandemic recovery period. Small businesses continue to face significant challenges.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Northern Star's Shares Plummet Following Output Guidance Cut
data.description: Northern Star Resources experienced its largest share decline in six years after the company reduced its production forecast. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the gold miner faced operational challenges at one of its processing mills, leading to the adjustment in output expectations. The company has been dealing with technical issues that have impacted its ability to meet previous production targets. This development has raised concerns among investors, contributing to the significant drop in share value. Northern Star is working to resolve the issues and aims to stabilize operations to restore investor confidence.

data.title: BitMex: The 20 millionth Bitcoin has been mined; institutional funds returning to BTC have driven the price up by 8.09%.
data.description: The 20 millionth Bitcoin has been mined, leaving only about 1 million Bitcoins to reach the 21 million hard cap. Since February 24th, influenced by the conflict in Iran, Bitcoin has risen 8.09%, while gold has fallen 0.42% during the same period, indicating a trend of funds rotating from gold to digital assets. In the US market, the Coinbase premium and continuous inflows into BTC ETFs suggest that US buyers are driving this rally. Large single-day inflows occurred between February 24th and March 4th, followed by a slight net outflow on March 5th and 6th, before the inflow trend resumed in the last two days. MicroStrategy Preferred Stock (STRC) has seen a premium for four consecutive trading days accompanied by record trading volume. This premium is estimated to have driven approximately $130 million to $180 million specifically for Bitcoin purchases over the past two days, roughly equivalent to 40% of a strong ETF inflow day. When the price of STRC is above $100, MicroStrategy can efficiently raise funds through a market offering and use them to purchase Bitcoin.

data.title: Nagasaki's Transformation Explored in New Adaptation of Ishiguro's Novel
data.description: A new adaptation of Kazuo Ishiguro's novel 'A Pale View of Hills' brings Nagasaki to life in vivid technicolor, offering a deep exploration of trauma, memory, and national reflection. Bloomberg posted on X, the adaptation delves into the complexities of post-war Japan, highlighting the emotional and psychological aftermath experienced by its characters. The narrative focuses on the themes of personal and collective memory, providing a poignant look at how individuals and nations come to terms with their past. This adaptation aims to capture the essence of Ishiguro's work, presenting a fresh perspective on the enduring impact of historical events on personal lives.
